The **BYD Shark 6** (also called the **BYD Shark** in some markets[[3]](#cite_note-3)) is a [plug-in hybrid](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plug-in_hybrid "Plug-in hybrid") [mid-size pickup truck](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mid-size_pickup_truck "Mid-size pickup truck") manufactured by the Chinese auto manufacturing company [BYD Auto](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BYD_Auto "BYD Auto"). The vehicle debuted in [Mexico](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") in May 2024 as the first light [utility vehicle](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Utility_vehicle "Utility vehicle") model made by BYD. It is based on a [body-on-frame](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Body-on-frame "Body-on-frame") chassis with a Dual Mode Off-Road (DMO) [hybrid drivetrain](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hybrid_vehicle_drivetrain "Hybrid vehicle drivetrain"), similar to the platform used on the [Denza B5](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denza_B5 "Denza B5").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In April 2024, BYD announced that it will be naming its first pickup truck the BYD Shark.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The BYD Shark was first introduced in Mexico on 14 May 2024. It is the first time BYD had a vehicle global debut launch outside China, as the BYD Shark is not marketed in China.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) The model is produced in China at BYD Super Intelligent Factory in [Zhengzhou, Henan](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zhengzhou "Zhengzhou").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

The BYD Shark later expanded into other international markets. It was launched in Brazil in October 2024 as part of BYD’s broader Latin American strategy.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) In Australia, the model—marketed as the BYD Shark 6—became available for order on 29 October 2024, with first deliveries commencing later that year.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Specifications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Specifications")]

The pickup truck was originally powered across all models by a 1.5-litre 135 kW (181 hp; 184 PS) [turbocharged](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turbocharged "Turbocharged") [petrol engine](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Petrol_engine "Petrol engine") mounted [longitudinally](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Longitudinal_engine "Longitudinal engine"), along with two [electric motors](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electric_motor "Electric motor"), one on each axle, the front motor is rated at 170 kW (228 hp; 231 PS), and the rear motor 150 kW (201 hp; 204 PS), creating an electric [four-wheel drive](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Four-wheel_drive "Four-wheel drive") layout rated at 321 kW (430 hp; 436 PS) and 650 N⋅m (479 lb⋅ft) of torque.[[12]](#cite_note-12) BYD claims that it is the world's first longitudinally-mounted range-extender hybrid layout.[[13]](#cite_note-:1-13) It uses BYD's proprietary [LFP](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lithium_iron_phosphate_battery "Lithium iron phosphate battery") [blade battery](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blade_battery "Blade battery") with a capacity of 29.58 kWh installed with a [cell](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electric_vehicle_battery "Electric vehicle battery")-to-[chassis](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vehicle_frame "Vehicle frame") technology mounted under the floor,[[14]](#cite_note-14) for an electric-only range of 85 km using the [WLTP](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Worldwide_Harmonised_Light_Vehicles_Test_Procedure "Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure") cycle and 670 km combined.[[15]](#cite_note-15) It is able to be charged on a fast [DC](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Direct_current "Direct current") charger at a rate of up to 55 kW.[[13]](#cite_note-:1-13)

The claimed [0-100 km/h acceleration](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/0_to_60_mph "0 to 60 mph") time is 5.7 seconds, and the [fuel consumption](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fuel_consumption "Fuel consumption") is claimed to be 7.5 L/100 km (13.3 km/l; 31.4 mpg‑US). It has a [braked towing capacity](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Braked_towing_capacity "Braked towing capacity") of 2,500 kg (5,510 lb), a 790 kg (1,740 lb) or 835 kg (1,840 lb) payload and 1,450 L (51 cu ft) cargo volume.[[16]](#cite_note-:0-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)

The Performance variant, first released in April 2026 in Australia, is powered by a 2.0-litre turbocharged petrol engine and two electric motors, producing a combined 350 kW (469 hp; 476 PS) and 700 N⋅m (516 lb⋅ft) of torque. It has a faster claimed 0-100km/h acceleration time of 5.5 seconds, and an increased braked towing capacity to 3,500 kg (7,716 lb).[[18]](#cite_note-quickerandmore-18)

The Dynamic variant is a [cab chassis](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cab_chassis "Cab chassis") designed specifically for the Australian market.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

The BYD Shark uses a coil spring [double wishbone](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Double_wishbone "Double wishbone") [independent](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Independent_suspension "Independent suspension") rear suspension instead of leaf spring commonly used by its competitors in the [mid-size](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mid-size_pickup_truck "Mid-size pickup truck") segment.[[20]](#cite_note-20) It has a claimed unladen [ground clearance](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ground_clearance "Ground clearance") figure of 230 mm (9.1 in). The [approach angle](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Approach_angle "Approach angle") is at 31 degrees, while the [ramp over](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rampover_angle "Rampover angle") and [departure angle](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Departure_angle "Departure angle") are 17 degrees and 19 degrees respectively.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

* [![Rear view](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/51/2025_BYD_Shark_6_rear.jpg/250px-2025_BYD_Shark_6_rear.jpg)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2025_BYD_Shark_6_rear.jpg "Rear view")

  Rear view
* [![Interior](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/26/BYD_Shark_6_DMO_AWD_interior.jpg/250px-BYD_Shark_6_DMO_AWD_interior.jpg)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:BYD_Shark_6_DMO_AWD_interior.jpg "Interior")

  Interior

## Equipment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Equipment")]

The cabin of the BYD Shark came equipped with full synthetic leather interior trimmings, a 12.8-inch or 15.6-inch touchscreen [infotainment](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/In-car_entertainment "In-car entertainment") with rotating screen feature, a 10.25-inch digital instrument cluster, and a 12-speaker [Dynaudio](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dynaudio "Dynaudio")-branded premium audio system,[[13]](#cite_note-:1-13) although in Latin America it is sold with a basic 8 speaker system without subwoofer.

## Markets

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Markets")]

### Africa

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Africa")]

#### South Africa

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: South Africa")]

The vehicle is marketed in South Africa as the **BYD Shark 6**. It went on sale in South Africa on 4 April 2025 in the sole Premium variant. At the time of its introduction, it became the most powerful pick-up truck on sale in South Africa.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

### Asia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Asia")]

#### Brunei

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Brunei")]

The vehicle is marketed in Brunei as the **BYD Shark 6**. It was introduced on 21 June 2025 in a single variant.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

#### Laos

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Laos")]

The vehicle is marketed in Laos as the **BYD Shark 6**. It was introduced in late November 2024 in a single variant.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

#### Philippines

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Philippines")]

The vehicle is marketed in the Philippines as the **BYD Shark 6**. It went on sale in the Philippines on 7 March 2025 in two trim levels: Advanced and Premium.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

#### Pakistan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Pakistan")]

The vehicle is marketed in Pakistan as the **BYD Shark 6**. It went on sale in Pakistan on 25 July 2025.

### Oceania

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Oceania")]

#### Australia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Australia")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e3/2026_BYD_Shark_6_Dynamic_-_Melbourne_Motor_Show_-_carsales.jpg/250px-2026_BYD_Shark_6_Dynamic_-_Melbourne_Motor_Show_-_carsales.jpg)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2026_BYD_Shark_6_Dynamic_-_Melbourne_Motor_Show_-_carsales.jpg)

BYD Shark 6 Dynamic at the 2026 [Melbourne Motor Show](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melbourne_Motor_Show "Melbourne Motor Show")

The vehicle is marketed in Australia as the **BYD Shark 6**. It went on sale in Australia in late October 2024 in a single variant, Premium.[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27) In April 2026 the cab chassis Dynamic variant went on sale, with a alloy tray designed with Ironman 4x4, a four-wheel-drive accessories specialist.[[28]](#cite_note-28) Also in April 2026 the Performance variant released, has increased performance and an additional "Crawl Mode" limiting the speed to 20 km/h (12 mph), to be added later to the Dynamic and Premium variants.[[18]](#cite_note-quickerandmore-18)[[29]](#cite_note-29)

In April 2026, BYD made its 100,000th sale in Australia, a BYD Shark 6 Premium.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

#### New Zealand

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: New Zealand")]

The vehicle is marketed in New Zealand as the **BYD Shark 6**. It went on sale in New Zealand in October 2024 in a single variant, Premium AWD.[[31]](#cite_note-31) In April 2026, the Dynamic Cab-Chassis and Performance variants were added to the line-up.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

### Americas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Americas")]

#### Brazil

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Brazil")]

The **BYD Shark** went on sale in Brazil in October 2024 in a single variant.[[33]](#cite_note-33)

#### Mexico

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Mexico")]

The Mexican market **BYD Shark** was introduced in May 2024. It is available in two trim levels, namely GL and GS.[[16]](#cite_note-:0-16)[[34]](#cite_note-34)

## Safety

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Safety")]

[ANCAP](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australasian_New_Car_Assessment_Program "Australasian New Car Assessment Program") test results  
 BYD SHARK 6  
(2025, aligned with [Euro NCAP](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euro_NCAP "Euro NCAP"))[[35]](#cite_note-35)

| Test | Points | % |
| Overall: |
| Adult occupant: | 35.37 | 85% |
| Child occupant: | 43 | 87% |
| Pedestrian: | 47.14 | 74% |
| Safety assist: | 15.59 | 86% |

## Motorsport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Motorsport")]

The Shark 6 competed and won the Frontera Aventura 2025 Rally in the Dominican Republic in March 2025.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

## Sales

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BYD_Shark_6&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Sales")]

In 2024, BYD exported 11,038 BYD Shark units from China.[[37]](#cite_note-37)

| Year | Australia | New Zealand |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2025 | 18,073[[38]](#cite_note-38) | 1,885[[39]](#cite_note-39) |

sources.title: BYD Shark Ute 6 launches in Australia with an introductory price of $57,990 | Zecar | Reviews | News
sources.content:
News

# BYD Shark Ute 6 launches in Australia with an introductory price of $57,990

[By Danny Thai](/authors/Danny-Thai)|October 29, 2024

![BYD Shark 6 three quarter view](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fo5m1ex7g%2Fproduction%2F8e6cf8fa633335cbca471161ffae0e58c08191cf-1600x895.jpg%3Fw%3D2000%26fit%3Dmin&w=1080&q=75 "BYD Shark 6 three quarter view")

![BYD Shark 6 front view](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fo5m1ex7g%2Fproduction%2F0316a403fad47447dc18326d953582a706c703e4-1600x1067.jpg%3Fw%3D2000%26fit%3Dmin&w=1080&q=75 "BYD Shark 6 front view")

The **2025 BYD Shark 6** plug-in hybrid (PHEV) Ute, BYD's answer to the Ford Ranger and Toyota Hilux – has entered showrooms with a special introductory price of $57,990 before on-road costs for the first 4000 units. The price will revert to the MSRP of $59,990 thereafter. The first 2000 orders will also receive a $500 credit towards BYD accessories.

It will make the Shark 6 comparable or lower in price to mid-specification dual-cab 4x4 utes such as the Ford Ranger XLS Bi-Turbo (from $57,630), Toyota HiLux SR5 manual ($60,670), Isuzu D-Max X-Rider (from $59,500), Mazda BT-50 XTR (from $59,280), Mitsubishi Triton GLS (from $59,090) and Nissan Navara ST-X (from $58,945)

The Shark 6 is BYD Australia's second [plug-in-hybrid model](https://zecar.com/resources/every-plug-in-hybrid-ev-phev-available-in-australia) joining the BYD Sealion 6 and fifth overall BYD model which includes the fully electric [Atto 3 SUV](https://zecar.com/resources/byd-atto-3-charging-guide), [Dolphin hatchback](https://zecar.com/reviews/2023-byd-dolphin-price-and-specs), and [Seal sedan](https://zecar.com/reviews/2024-byd-seal-price-and-spec).

The Shark 6 will launch with a top-of-the-range Premium variant, built on the company’s DMO (Dual Mode Offroad) platform, it features a 1.5-litre turbo-petrol, dual-motor all-wheel drive powertrain.

The Shark 6 will be the the fastest accelerating ute in the market, capable of accelerating from zero to 100km/h in a claimed 5.7 seconds. It will offer a pure electric range of 100km (NEDC) or ~80km based on the WLTP standard and a combined NEDC range in hybrid mode of 840km

Orders for the Shark 6 open today with deliveries expected in December or January next year.

## Size and Chassis

The BYD Shark 6 measures 5,457 mm in length, 1,971 mm in width and 1,925 mm in height, with a wheelbase of 3,260 mm. By comparison this makes it slightly larger than both the the popular Ford Ranger and Toyota Hilux in every dimension.

The Shark 6 has a payload rating of 835kg, a rear tray capacity of 1450L, and a 2500kg tow rating. Which is 1000kg lower than the 3500kg benchmark for the segment.

BYD claims the rear tray measures in at 1520mm long, 1500mm wide, and 517mm deep, with a total capacity of 1200L. This is broadly in line with the the Ford Ranger.

For off-road driving, it rides on 282mm of ground clearance and extensive underbody protection.

The Dual Mode Off-road (DMO) Super Hybrid Platform boasts multiple innovative features including the Cell to Chassis (CTC) technology, which enables the ideal balance between vehicle safety, powerful off-roading, and energy consumption.

## Key Specifications: Range, Charging

The BYD Shark 6 drivetrain is underpinned by BYD's proprietary [DM-i (Dual Motor Intelligence).](https://www.byd.com/eu/technology/byd-super-dm-plug-in-hybrid-technology)

The DMi-i is a variation of a PHEV technology which can operate in three power supply modes:

* Pure EV Mode - In Pure EV Mode, the wheels are 100% driven by the electric motor for an all-electric drive experience, with the engine on standby.
* HEV Series Mode - When the battery falls below a set state-of-charge, the engine acts as a generator charging the battery, the wheels are driven by the electric motor, making HEV mode the ideal companion for extending range.
* HEV Parallel Mode - The wheels are driven by both the electric motor and the engine.

Unlike other PHEVs however, the BYD Shark 6’s hybrid system prioritises electric propulsion, with the engine set-up to run as a range extender to charge the battery in most situations.

On average BYD claim the car will be powered by the electric motor in 90% of driving conditions.

The Shark 6 comes equipped with from dual motors capable of a combined **320kW** in power and **650Nm** of torque. The front motor generates 170 kW and 310 Nm of torque, while the rear motor adds 150 kW and 340 Nm of torque. This results in swift acceleration, reaching 0 to 100 km/h in just **5.7 seconds**. The top speed is listed at 160 km/h.

The **30kWh battery** provides a pure electric range of **100km** (based on the NEDC) or ~80km based on the WLTP standard. The electric only range should be sufficient to cover most daily trips in Australia (40km/day).

Using a 7kW AC charger, the Shark 6's battery can be charged from 30-80 per cent in two hours, a 55kW DC fast charger can deliver the same energy in 25 minutes.

The combined NEDC range in hybrid mode is **840km**. Additionally, the hybrid system offers a fuel efficiency of 7.5 litres per 100km.

## Key Technology and Features

The interior features a range of high-tech amenities similar to what we are accustomed to in the [BYD EV range](https://zecar.com/reviews/what-byd-electric-cars-are-coming-to-australia). Features include a 10.25-inch LCD instrument cluster, a large 12.8-inch central touchscreen, and a 12-inch head-up display (HUD) for easy access to information. Connectivity is well-covered with support for both Apple CarPlay and Android Auto.

Available technology features include a 360-degree camera system, head-up display, wireless smartphone charging, air purification system, and Apple CarPlay for iPhone users. Owners can also use a [smartphone app](https://zecar.com/resources/what-mobile-apps-do-you-need-for-your-ev) to remotely control cabin temperature and charging.

The Shark 6 will feature [vehicle-to-load (V2L)](https://zecar.com/resources/watt-is-bidirectional-charging-v2g-v2h-v2l) technology which will enable power tools and appliances to be powered from the truck's 30 kWh battery pack.

## BYD Shark 6 Price

The BYD Shark 6 will launch in a single trim for $57,990 before on-road costs for the first 4000 units and $59,990 plus on-road costs thereafter.

By comparison, the BYD Shark Premium sells in Mexico for 969,800 pesos ($A86,697/$57,589)
